mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 8.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in less than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE drops below 7.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue guidance and equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or flat 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of observations, or single-series ICs dominate the interaction term in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports equity margins over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum shows zero correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across all rolling 3-year windows, or the signal flips direction during supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms economic expansion, which historically precedes earnings upgrades and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDP growth crosses the 2.0 percent threshold, or the signal generates losses during high-inflation growth spurts.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates a healthy wage-price equilibrium, which sustains consumer spending power and supports equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term becomes statistically insignificant or negative in a multivariate regression, or SPX forward returns decline when payroll growth outpaces inflation by more than 0.5 percent monthly.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in retail sales volume reflects sustained consumer expenditure, which directly lifts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-month Rank IC for RSAFS momentum against 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02, or the signal fails to predict returns during seasonal demand shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous federal funds rate hikes and rising consumer price inflation compress real yields and trigger valuation multiple contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand despite rising real rates and inflation, or the joint signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of historical instances.
